channel.title: Orthodox Christian Sermons: 25th Sunday after Pentecost
channel.description: Audio Orthodox Christian Sermons: 25th Sunday after Pentecost

item.itunes:summary: :25th Sunday after Pentecost. The Parable Of The Good Samaritan, 2002

item.title: 25th Sunday after Pentecost, Parable of the Good Samaritan, Who is my neighbor?, 2008-12-07
item.description: :25th Sunday after Pentecost. Parable of the Good Samaritan, Who is my neighbor?, 2008-12-07
item.itunes:author: Priest Seraphim Holland
item.itunes:summary: :25th Sunday after Pentecost. Parable of the Good Samaritan, Who is my neighbor?, 2008-12-07

item.title: 25th Sunday after Pentecost, The Greatest Commandment and Parable of the Good Samaritan, Two answers to the question who is my neighbor, 2009-11-29
item.description: :25th Sunday after Pentecost. Regarding the question of "Who is my neighbor", the Lord Jesus Christ, as is usual, gives many answers at once. The parable of the Good Samaritan is rich in symbolism and Theology, and teaches about morality, the church, the second coming and the judgment, but it basically boils down to two broad "answers": we must know God and know ourselves. This parable, more than any other in Scripture, shows the ministry of the incarnate God-man Jesus Christ and the nature of man. If we understand both, we will always know who our neighbor is.
item.itunes:author: Priest Seraphim Holland
item.itunes:summary: :25th Sunday after Pentecost. Regarding the question of "Who is my neighbor", the Lord Jesus Christ, as is usual, gives many answers at once. The parable of the Good Samaritan is rich in symbolism and Theology, and teaches about morality, the church, the second coming and the judgment, but it basically boils down to two broad "answers": we must know God and know ourselves. This parable, more than any other in Scripture, shows the ministry of the incarnate God-man Jesus Christ and the nature of man. If we understand both, we will always know who our neighbor is.

item.title: 25th Sunday after Pentecost, One Body, One Spirit, One Hope, One Lord, One Faith, One Baptism, Descending,then Ascending Fulfills The Gospel, 2010-11-14
item.description: :25th Sunday after Pentecost. In this longer than usual sermon, the first part of Ephesians chapter 4 is expounded on. This selection not only presents important dogmas that are the bedrock of our faith, it also presents the proper perspective to have regarding our neighbor - a perspective that will help us to fulfill all the commandments. The doctrine of Christ's descent into Hell is discussed, and its moral application to all of our prayer, and indeed, all of our life. This selection contains some of the most important spiritual lessons a pastor can preach.
item.itunes:author: Priest Seraphim Holland
item.itunes:summary: :25th Sunday after Pentecost. In this longer than usual sermon, the first part of Ephesians chapter 4 is expounded on. This selection not only presents important dogmas that are the bedrock of our faith, it also presents the proper perspective to have regarding our neighbor - a perspective that will help us to fulfill all the commandments. The doctrine of Christ's descent into Hell is discussed, and its moral application to all of our prayer, and indeed, all of our life. This selection contains some of the most important spiritual lessons a pastor can preach.

item.title: 25th Sunday after Pentecost, Parable Of The Good Samaritan, 8th Sunday of Luke. Without empathy you will not be saved. , 2010-11-28
item.description: :25th Sunday after Pentecost. The very important Parable of the Good Samaritan, only in Luke is always read in or near the Nativity Fast, appropriately, since it may be the finest exposition of incarnational theology in the Gospels. The answer to the question teaches us a critical virtue - empathy with our fellow man, without which we will not be saved, by describing the whole economy of the incarnation in the symbolism of the elements of the parable. Truly, this is a parable we should contemplate deeply. This parable is read on the 25th Sunday after Pentecost, or in some years when the Lukan jump is employed before this Sunday, on whatever Sunday is the 8th Sunday of Luke. In this year (2010), it was read on the 27th Sunday after Pentecost, which was the first day of the Nativity fast.
item.itunes:author: Priest Seraphim Holland
item.itunes:summary: :25th Sunday after Pentecost. The very important Parable of the Good Samaritan, only in Luke is always read in or near the Nativity Fast, appropriately, since it may be the finest exposition of incarnational theology in the Gospels. The answer to the question teaches us a critical virtue - empathy with our fellow man, without which we will not be saved, by describing the whole economy of the incarnation in the symbolism of the elements of the parable. Truly, this is a parable we should contemplate deeply. This parable is read on the 25th Sunday after Pentecost, or in some years when the Lukan jump is employed before this Sunday, on whatever Sunday is the 8th Sunday of Luke. In this year (2010), it was read on the 27th Sunday after Pentecost, which was the first day of the Nativity fast.

item.title: 25th Sunday after Pentecost, 2011-11-25
item.description: :25th Sunday after Pentecost. What must I do to be saved? Follow the Greatest commandment and understand how to do this by understanding the Parable of the Good Samaritan.
item.itunes:author: Priest Seraphim Holland
item.itunes:summary: :25th Sunday after Pentecost. What must I do to be saved? Follow the Greatest commandment and understand how to do this by understanding the Parable of the Good Samaritan.
